description: Fix React Error #310 (infinite re-render loop), fix multi-device realtime sync, fix project switching, and add triple-click logo access to project panel
steps: Step 1 - Break the circular save loop (lines ~873-930 of src/App.jsx): The useEffect at line ~902 watches [workspaces, activeTab, nextId, initialized, activeProjectId] and calls setProjects(prev => prev.map(...)) which updates the projects state. Meanwhile, the realtime subscription (line ~796) also calls setProjects when it receives incoming changes. The setProjects call in the sync effect does NOT directly trigger re-renders that loop back (React batches), BUT the REAL infinite loop is: (1) workspaces change -> (2) useEffect at ~873 triggers debouncedSaveToCloud -> (3) cloud save completes -> (4) realtime subscription receives the echo -> (5) despite deviceId check, the 2-second timestamp fallback allows some through -> (6) realtime handler calls setWorkspaces -> (7) workspaces change -> loop repeats. FIX: In the realtime subscription handler, when receiving an update for the activeProjectId, set skipNextSaveRef.current = true BEFORE calling setWorkspaces/setActiveTab/setNextId. This prevents the cloud-save useEffect from firing on incoming remote data. Also, remove the fragile 2-second timestamp fallback as the secondary filter - rely solely on deviceId for self-echo suppression.

Step 2 - Fix the useEffect at line ~902 that syncs workspace state into projects array: This effect calls setProjects every time workspaces/activeTab/nextId change. While this doesn't directly cause an infinite loop (setProjects updating projects doesn't trigger any effect that changes workspaces), it IS called on EVERY workspace change including those from realtime. When the realtime handler updates workspaces, this effect then calls setProjects AND triggers the debounced cloud save (from the effect at ~873). FIX: The cloud-save useEffect at ~873 already has skipNextSaveRef logic. Ensure the realtime handler sets skipNextSaveRef.current = true before updating workspaces. Additionally, in the setProjects sync effect at ~902, use a ref-based comparison to avoid redundant setProjects calls - only update projects if the workspaces/activeTab/nextId actually differ from what's already stored in the project.

Step 3 - Fix multi-device realtime sync to work for ANY project: Currently the realtime subscription (line ~796) has activeProjectId in its dependency array, meaning it re-subscribes every time the active project changes. The handler correctly updates the projects array for any incoming project, and only updates rendered workspaces if incomingProjectId === activeProjectId. The issue is the useEffect dependency on activeProjectId causes channel teardown/recreation on project switch which can miss messages. FIX: Remove activeProjectId from the useEffect dependency array. Instead, use a ref (activeProjectIdRef) that is always kept in sync, and check activeProjectIdRef.current inside the handler. This prevents channel reconnection on project switch.

Step 4 - Fix project creation not showing the switch: In the createProject function (line ~1437), after creating the project, the function only calls setProjects and saves to cloud but does NOT switch to the new project or update the UI to show it's been created. The user wants to see the project in the 'open' panel. The current code already sets setProjectPanelMode('open') which should show the list. Verify this works. If the issue is that the project list doesn't update visually because of stale closure over projects, ensure the setProjects updater pattern is used correctly. The current code uses setProjects(prev => [...prev, newProj]) which is correct. The real UX issue is the user expects to be switched to the new project after creation. FIX: After creating a project, automatically switch to it by calling the same logic as cycleToProject but without password check (since we just created it).

Step 5 - Ensure triple-click on logo works reliably: The handleLogoTap function (line ~1420) already implements triple-click detection with a 1000ms window and count >= 3. Verify the onClick handler is attached to the logo element (confirmed at line ~3178). This appears to already work. Ensure it's robust by also resetting the timer if the interval between any two clicks exceeds 500ms (tighter window to avoid accidental triggers).

Step 6 - Ensure export covers ALL projects: The exportData function (line ~1648) already exports all projects with version 2 format. Verify the import function (handleImport at line ~1670 and the project panel import at ~2853) both handle version 2 multi-project imports. Both appear to already handle this correctly. Ensure the project panel's export/import section works properly.
Step 7 - Verify build succeeds: Run npm install and npm run build to confirm no compilation errors after all changes.
acceptance_criteria: npm run build completes without errors
The useEffect that saves to cloud does NOT fire when receiving remote realtime updates (skipNextSaveRef is set before setWorkspaces calls from realtime handler)
The realtime subscription does NOT re-subscribe when activeProjectId changes (uses ref instead of state in dependency array)
Self-echo suppression relies on deviceId check only - the 2-second timestamp fallback is removed or made secondary
Creating a new project automatically switches to it
Triple-click on the logo icon opens the project panel
verification: Run 'npm install && npm run build' in /projects/sandbox/mind-map2 and confirm exit code 0

Read the modified src/App.jsx and verify: (1) realtime handler sets skipNextSaveRef.current = true before calling setWorkspaces, (2) realtime useEffect dependency array does not include activeProjectId, (3) createProject function switches to the new project after creation
findings: All 5 bugs addressed. Build passes successfully. Bug 5 (export/import) confirmed already working - no changes needed. The infinite re-render loop was caused by: (1) the 2-second timestamp fallback letting self-echoes through, and (2) missing skipNextSaveRef before setWorkspaces in realtime handler. The realtime subscription re-subscribe issue was solved by using a ref for activeProjectId and removing it from the dependency array. Follow-up review fixes applied: (1) skipNextSaveRef renamed to skipSaveCountRef and converted from boolean to counter for robustness against non-batched renders, (2) duplicated project-switch logic extracted into switchToProjectDirect helper used by createProject, switchProject, and cycleToProject.
